spec_match.rationale: The page uses containertiming attributes, observes PerformanceObserver entry type "container" when supported, and displays identifier, render time, first render time context, and size-oriented timing behavior.

interactive.rationale: The user can replay observation, switch paint scenarios, and toggle containertiming-ignore to see live timing rows change.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept lede and feature-index blurb both describe timing annotated sections through PerformanceObserver entries.

edge_cases.rationale: Unsupported browsers get an explicit fallback message and the containertiming-ignore toggle demonstrates an ignored subtree edge case.

references.rationale: A labelled references section cites chromestatus.com/feature/5110962817073152 and the Container Timing draft.

design_system.rationale: The inline styles use shared CSS variables and button background overrides also set readable foreground colors.

summary: The major interactivity finding is resolved: the page now has live controls, correct Container Timing observer semantics, visible fallback handling, and a labelled references section.
